Title: Evolution of the Chalcone Isomerase Fold from Fatty Acid-Binding to Stereospecific Enzyme

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtFAP1 is a non-catalytic CHI-fold protein that binds fatty acids in vitro rather than catalyzing chalcone isomerization. 2. AtFAP1 localizes to plastid stroma and is highly expressed in developing and reproductive tissues, co-expressed with fatty-acid biosynthetic genes. 3. atfap1 null mutants show elevated total fatty acids, especially α-linolenic acid (C18:3), plus reproductive defects including shorter siliques, abnormal ovules, and reduced seed set.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane lipid composition, storage lipid accumulation, and normal reproductive/embryo development
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes reproductive defects, shorter siliques, abnormal ovules, reduced viable seed yield, and increased fatty-acid accumulation; temperature-dependent phenotypes in leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning seed and leaf fatty-acid composition, but manipulation may compromise fertility and seed yield; requires caution in breeding or engineering
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seeds at 6 days after flowering; developing cotyledons, young seedlings, roots, seeds, embryos, macrospores, preanthesis and tapetum; restricted mainly to developing and reproductive tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth temperature (15°C, 22°C, 26°C) strongly affects fatty-acid phenotype
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AtFAP1 increases total fatty-acid content and elevates α-linolenic acid (C18:3), with associated reproductive defects
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome co-expression analysis from 72 microarray experiments using MetOmGraph
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seeds at 6 days after flowering; developing cotyledons, young seedlings, roots, seeds, embryos, macrospores, preanthesis and tapetum
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants; GFP localization; GUS promoter-reporter expression; HPLC-MS ligand analysis; Thermofluor fatty-acid binding assay; GC-MS fatty-acid profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure showed bound lauric acid molecules in the pocket; AtFAP1 can sequester exogenously supplied α-linolenic acid, suggesting preference for unsaturated C18 fatty acid in planta despite saturation-biased binding observed from expression host-derived ligands

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtFAP2 is a non-catalytic CHI-fold fatty acid-binding protein localized to plastids. 2. It is expressed broadly throughout plant life and in developing tissues linked to fatty-acid metabolism. 3. atfap2 null mutants alone are largely indistinguishable from wild type during vegetative growth, but double-mutant combinations show additive developmental effects, supporting a role overlapping with other FAPs in fatty-acid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lipid metabolism associated with development and seed biology
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single loss-of-function phenotype appears weak, but combined perturbation with other FAP genes contributes to additive developmental effects
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for multigene manipulation of fatty-acid traits, but likely requires stacking with other FAP genes to produce strong effects
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Developing cotyledons, young seedlings, roots, seeds, embryos, macrospores, preanthesis and tapetum; expressed throughout the life of the plant
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports fatty-acid metabolic/developmental functions, with additive effects in double-mutant backgrounds
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome co-expression context mentioned for FAP family; specific AtFAP2 co-expression detail limited
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing cotyledons, young seedlings, roots, seeds, embryos, macrospores, preanthesis and tapetum; throughout plant life
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants; GFP localization; GUS promoter-reporter expression; HPLC-MS ligand analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtFAP2 was recalcitrant to crystallization, but HPLC-MS confirmed association with saturated fatty acids

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtFAP3 is a plastid-localized, non-catalytic CHI-fold fatty acid-binding protein phylogenetically closest to the ancestor of vascular-plant CHI. 2. AtFAP3 binds fatty acids in vitro, with broad interaction across tested fatty acids and highest relative affinity for C16:0, while structural data suggest preference for cis-unsaturated C16/C18 fatty acids. 3. RNAi knockdown alters fatty-acid composition and increases fatty-acid content in leaves and seeds, while also causing strong vegetative and reproductive developmental defects.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage lipid accumulation, membrane composition, and normal plant/embryo development
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown causes early bolting, fast growth, increased branching, reduced apical dominance, reduced plant height, and reproductive defects; no strong knockdown/knockout recovered, suggesting essential developmental function
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying fatty-acid content/composition, but major developmental penalties and probable essentiality make it a high-risk engineering target
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seeds at 6 days after flowering; developing cotyledons, young seedlings, roots, seeds, embryos, macrospores, preanthesis and tapetum; expression strongly enriched in developing and reproductive tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth temperature influences fatty-acid phenotypes in FAP mutant backgrounds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduction of AtFAP3 alters fatty-acid composition and increases fatty-acid content in leaves and seeds, with severe developmental defects
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome co-expression analysis from 72 microarray experiments using MetOmGraph
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seeds at 6 days after flowering; developing cotyledons, young seedlings, roots, seeds, embryos, macrospores, preanthesis and tapetum
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; GFP localization; GUS promoter-reporter expression; X-ray crystallography; HPLC-MS ligand analysis; Thermofluor fatty-acid binding assay; GC-MS fatty-acid profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure showed bound palmitic acid; phylogeny indicates CHI evolved from the FAP3 lineage

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: core flavanone-forming step of flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes intramolecular and stereospecific cyclization of chalcones to chiral (S)-flavanones via a Michael addition reaction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid biosynthesis; mutation abolishes flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCHI is a bona fide catalytic CHI enzyme in Arabidopsis. 2. It converts chalcones to (2S)-naringenin in vitro as a committed step in flavonoid biosynthesis. 3. Mutation of AtCHI causes plants devoid of flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate flavanone product required for downstream flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; sequence/active-site analysis; mutant phenotype referenced
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzymatic target for flavonoid metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as enzymatic reference against non-catalytic FAP and CHIL relatives; PDB accession 4DOI
